title: Chinese Chicken Soup (Community Enhanced)
original_instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add chile paste, ginger, and turmeric; cook and stir until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes.
Stir in chicken broth, chicken, celery, soy sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil; add noodles and simmer for 3 minutes.
Stir in lettuce and remove from heat. Garnish servings with green onions.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add chile paste, ginger, and turmeric; cook and stir until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes.

Boil 1 cup water. Add thinly cut celery, turmeric, ginger, chili paste and boil for 1 minute. Add remaining broth and soy sauce. Once heated, add chicken. Bring to a boil. Add 1 cup chopped cabbage (2 cups if desired) and thin spaghetti. Cook until pasta is tender, about 6 minutes. Stir in sesame oil and green onions.
Stir in lettuce and remove from heat. Garnish servings with green onions.

line_diffs.reasoning: Reducing the amount of sesame oil can help balance the flavors in the soup
Using chicken thighs instead of chicken breast can add more flavor and tenderness to the soup
Adding carrots, yellow onion, and frozen chicken/veggie wontons enhances the flavor and texture of the soup

line_diffs.original: Stir in chicken broth, chicken, celery, soy sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil; add noodles and simmer for 3 minutes.

Add 1 cup chopped cabbage (2 cups if desired) and thin spaghetti. Cook until pasta is tender, about 6 minutes. Stir in sesame oil and green onions.
line_diffs.reasoning: Altering the cooking method by boiling celery, turmeric, ginger, and chili paste in water first may enhance the flavor profile of the soup

line_diffs.original: Stir in chicken broth, chicken, celery, soy sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil; add noodles and simmer for 3 minutes.

source_review.text: This soup was so good! I cooked chicken thighs in sesame oil and turmeric, then chopped them. I added some carrots and yellow onion and used cabbage instead of lettuce. Delicious!*Edit* I made it again and would also reduce the amount of sesame oil. I also put some frozen chicken/veggie wontons in it and my son loved it!

modifications_applied.reasoning: Using cabbage instead of lettuce adds a different texture and flavor to the soup

source_review.text: 11.17.20 ... https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/23878/chinese-chicken-soup/ ... Chinese Chicken Soup was quite good! I barely changed the base, which really decides the soup's success. 'Used homemade saltfree chicken broth; halved the ginger; subbed shredded cabbage for the lettuce. 'Changed directions. Me: Boiled 1c water. Added thinly cut celery, tumeric, ginger, chili paste & boiled 1 minute. Added remaining broth & soy sauce. Once heated, added chicken. Brought to a boil. Added 1c chopped cabbage (2c, if again) & thin spaghetti. Cooked until pasta was tender, about 6 minutes. Stirred in sesame oil & green onions. 'Could have added the sugar, but added an additional cup of broth due to spicy/salt factor. 'Looks like a restaurant's offering. :)

modifications_applied.reasoning: Altering the cooking method by boiling celery, turmeric, ginger, and chili paste in water first may enhance the flavor profile of the soup

enhancement_summary.expected_impact: Using cabbage instead of lettuce adds a different texture and flavor to the soup; Adding carrots, yellow onion, and frozen chicken/veggie wontons enhances the flavor and texture of the soup; Using chicken thighs instead of chicken breast can add more flavor and tenderness to the soup (and 2 more improvements)

description: This easy Chinese chicken soup with noodles is flavored with turmeric, chile paste, ginger, and soy for a quick and flavorsome meal in one pot.
